reasoning: The only realistic candidate for a Chinese military base in a BRICS country before 2026 is the UAE, where US intelligence previously detected construction of a suspected military facility at Khalifa Port, which was paused and reportedly resumed. However, the UAE maintains deep security ties with the US, and Washington has exerted immense pressure to prevent a Chinese military presence there. Given this pushback, it is highly unlikely the UAE will allow a full-fledged Chinese military base to become operational by 2025. Other BRICS countries either have constitutional bans (Iran), geopolitical rivalry (India), deep US military ties (Egypt), or lack any advanced construction indicators that would allow a base to open before 2026.
